mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which compresses input costs and yields positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative 5-day forward returns during periods of inventory overhang across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above threshold levels forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy stances, which increases borrowing costs for corporations and suppresses S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 265.0 consistently coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns when wage growth outpaces productivity growth by more than 1.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines over a 10-year window.
